ops.text:
CLOSE — VERDICT: CONFIRMED.

Independent bootstrap/MC regeneration finished (seed 8675309, B=20000). All 6 stochastic re-estimates land within Monte-Carlo noise of E15's stored B=2000 values (all slightly high, within the ~2.6% SE-of-SE band at B=2000): row-strat 0.010011 vs 0.009955 (+0.56%), row-unstrat 0.010007 vs 0.009787 (+2.26%), semiparam-n1325 0.006714 vs 0.006651 (+0.95%), label-flip 0.005664 vs 0.005619 (+0.79%), label-drop 0.003385 vs 0.003378 (+0.22%), joint 0.010287 vs 0.010070 (+2.16%). Recomputing MDE with my more-precise estimates: 0.04557 vs 0.04529 (+0.6%), delta/MDE 0.0197 vs 0.0199 — no conclusion moves.

Every DETERMINISTIC number matched to full float precision: AUC, HM/DeLong (both frames), additive+scaled totals, all 4 MDE values, all 5 rho-needed reclassifications (0.9996/0.9761/0.9858/0.9926/0.9017), all votes kappa/flip/disagreement, shrinkage rule (K=5). Gate frame verified from parquet: n=1325 (1032/293), shared_negatives=293 exactly (0 shared positives) — confirms the "293-neg minority is the bottleneck in both frames" claim structurally. Stored 7 npy draws std==reported SE to <1e-6.

Zero material discrepancies. No LLM spend ($0). Artifacts: out/recompute.py, out/fast_checks.py, out/results.json, out/recompute_stdout.txt, out/VERDICT.md.
